text: Remedy Aura (Passive) This passive provides you and nearby allies with healing. As such, try to position yourself where you can make the most use from it, without putting yourself in deadly situations. Try to keep maximum aura as much as possible, as it provides the most healing. This can mean attacking Deployables , such as Barriers , after a fight to keep the charge up.   Spirit Glaive As his primary source of damage, utilize Gpirit Glaive as often as possible to build and keep his aura at high energy. When headshotting with Spirit Glaive, the 30 impact damage will be doubled but the 30 damage over time will not. This makes the headshot multiplier effectively 1.5x, and bodyshotting a more valuable tool to build aura. The damage over time applied by Spirit Glaive pierces armor 's damage reduction, making it efficient against heroes with a lot of armor health. Familiarize yourself with the projectile's bouncing trajectory, as it can be used to attack around corners without you needing direct LOS on the enemy. The projectile can be shot underneath payload to hit attackers hiding on the other side.   Healing Kasa This is your primary source of burst healing, use it whenever an ally is critical. As each bounce reduces the amount of healing received, prioritize your lowest health ally first. You can indirectly target a teammate out of your LOS by targeting a closer teammate who does have LOS. Consider keeping your second kasa as burst heal for critical teammates, instead of using it to top up teammates that could be healed by aura or your other support.   Katashiro Return As one of Mizuki's strongest abilities, it allows him to be mobile and get right in the fight, then escape if necessary. As this ability provides a movement boost to Mizuki while active, be sure to use it as mobility to quickly reposition or get back to the fight, not just as an escape tool. Be wary if you use Binding Chain or any other ability just before the grace period ends to return. Being locked in the cast animation may prevent you from returning in time. A standard strategy is to use this ability to quickly go directly into the enemy team, hindering an opponent and dealing damage to build/maintain your aura, then returning back once the grace period ends. If doing this, be sure to position your paper doll somewhere safe where opponents won't instantly see you return to it, as you lose your escape tool doing this.   Binding Chain This is Mizuki's primary utility, allowing him to tether and hinder whoever is hit for a small period. As an extended melee attack, similar to Brigitte 's Whip Shot , it pierces Negate Projectile abilities that don't block melee attacks, making it more applicable than Cassidy 's Flashbang . Despite this, it's a lot harder to land than Flashbang, as it's not got an area of effect aspect to it. It's best used against dive/flank orientated heroes, such as Tracer or Doomfist , who become much more vulnerable when hindered after trying to dive/flank. Its cooldown is fairly long, so make sure you are using it on high value targets. This can mean something as simple as hitting an overextending tank, so your team can collectively punish them in the brief period they're immobile. It can also be used to prevent a enemy from making it to cover once they have reached critical health. Many dive enemies expect to engage your team with an ability that allows them to escape before they can be killed, such as Doomfist and Winston . By chaining them just as they turn around to disengage, you might keep them in your team's LOS long enough for you to get the kill. It can be used against flying heroes, such as Pharah or Jetpack Cat , to drag them to the ground where hit & make them easy targets. This can also be useful against heroes with vertical mobility, like D.Va and Baptiste , who are trying to escape to high-ground in the middle of a fight. Be careful when attempting this though, as they can be a difficult target to hit mid-air, albeit very rewarding. If you're going in with Katashiro Return , binding an opponent first can make them easier to hit (and hopefully eliminate) with Spirit Glaive .   Kekkai Sanctuary This is a uniquely difficult ultimate to work with, compared to other Support ultimate's, so timing is key. Its primary defense is not the healing it provides, but rather the projectile absorption of the perimeter, functioning similar to Defense Matrix . Although the healing provided isn't strong, it's better than nothing: don't be afraid to utilize the ultimate as a last-ditch effort to restore your team's health. Utilize the long-range available during placement to position the ultimate on top of your allies, so they can receive the instant healing. This ultimate can be used both defensively, to shield allies from enemy attacks, or offensively, to entrap opponents and prevent their supports from healing them. If used defensively, the short duration means timing and placement of the ultimate is important, to prevent the most incoming damage and ensure the damage is prevented, as projectiles are only negated if entering the area. As it functions similarly to Defense Matrix , it can be used against projectile-based ultimates such as Captive Sun or Deadeye , to completely negate them. Try position the ultimate such that the enemy cannot enter the area, as if they do then their attacks will not be negated. Also take note of which heroes the opponent is using. Beam , Melee and Shockwave attacks won't be negated. So, heroes like Zarya or Reinhardt will still be able to use most of their kit to attack you without needing to enter the area. If used offensively, it's best to coordinate with your allies before the opponent has the opportunity to exit the area. Be wary of what Supports the enemy team has, as some can provide healing through the projectile negation (such as Brigitte or Mercy ). If available, binding the opponent can keep them entrapped for a longer time, giving your team more opportunity to finish off the opponent. Be careful not to try to trap an opponent who can easily fend for themselves, such as Roadhog or Orisa , who can survive easily without their supports while trapped. Similarly, don't trap opponents who can easily escape, such as Winston or Doomfist , who can simply use their high mobility to exit before taking too much damage.
